title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
baseline_evidence: Baseline agents without Reflexion; GPT-4 single-sample code generation; CoT / episodic-memory ablations; previous SOTA systems such as CodeT and CodeRL
performance_evidence: HumanEval Python improves from GPT-4 80.1% pass@1 to 91.0% (+10.9pp); HumanEval Rust improves from GPT-4 60.0% to 68.0% (+8.0pp); LeetcodeHard Python improves from GPT-4 7.5% to 15.0% (+7.5pp).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Self-Taught Optimizer (STOP): Recursively Self-Improving Code Generation
baseline_evidence: Seed improver I0; chain-of-thought baseline; greedy iterative improver; GPT-3.5 and Mixtral comparisons
performance_evidence: Improved improvers outperform the seed improver on all transfer tasks reported in Table 1; on LPN, the paper reports consistent mean downstream performance improvement with GPT-4.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Empowering Agile-Based Generative Software Development through Human-AI Teamwork
baseline_evidence: AutoGPT, ChatDev_23, ChatDev_24, GPT-Engineer, GPT3.5+CoT, GPT4+CoT, GPT4o+CoT, ablations
performance_evidence: AgileGen GPT3.5 outperforms ChatDev_24 GPT3.5 by +0.089 CodeBLEU and +16.4pp Pass@1; AgileGen GPT4 outperforms GPT4o+CoT by +0.105 CodeBLEU and +24.7pp Pass@1.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: FALCON: Feedback-driven Adaptive Long/short-term memory reinforced Coding OptimizatioN
baseline_evidence: CodeT5 base, PPOCoder, CodeRL, RLTF, DeepSeek-Coder-Instruct without FALCON
performance_evidence: HumanEval improves from RLTF 76.8 to 82.9 (+6.1pp); MBPP improves from RLTF 75.9 to 80.7 (+4.8pp); APPS all pass@1 improves from RLTF 3.27 to 3.50 (+0.23pp).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Iterative Experience Refinement of Software-Developing Agents
baseline_evidence: GPTEngineer, MetaGPT, ChatDev, ECL (Experiential Co-Learning)
performance_evidence: +36.6% Quality vs ChatDev (0.6372 vs 0.4665); +10.3% Quality vs ECL (0.6372 vs 0.5775); +11% Executability vs ECL (0.9146 vs 0.8643)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence does not document a no-persistence or non-memory baseline suitable for the controlled-comparison denominator.

title: LLMs as Continuous Learners: Improving the Reproduction of Defective Code in Software Issues
baseline_evidence: CodeR, LIBRO, AutoCodeRover, Agentless, and versions with EvoCoder reproduction code
performance_evidence: Compared with CodeR, reproduction accuracy improves from 34.00% to 54.00% LLM-judged (+20.00pp) and from 33.33% to 53.33% human-judged (+20.00pp). AutoCodeRover fix rate improves from 22.06% to 26.47%; Agentless from 23.53% to 26.47%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Skill-Adpative Imitation Learning for UI Test Reuse
baseline_evidence: CraftDroid, SemFinder, CraftDroid-SemFinder, CraftDroid-DeepSeek-V2, CraftDroid-GPT-4o-mini, TraceDroid, TargetDroid
performance_evidence: SAIL-DeepSeek-V2 improves over CraftDroid 25.5% to 63.6% overall (+38.1pp; reported as 149% higher SR). In non-1-to-1 mappings, SAIL-GPT-4o reaches 55.6% versus CraftDroid 14.1% (reported as 294% improvement).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: A Self-Improving Coding Agent
baseline_evidence: Initial archived agent at iteration 0 and later self-improved agent iterations; paper also compares against ADAS conceptually.
performance_evidence: SWE-Bench Verified improves from 17% to 53% at the best reported iteration (+36pp). File editing improves from 0.82 to 0.96 at peak, symbol location from 0.35 to 0.43 at peak, and LiveCodeBench from 0.65 to 0.71 by iteration 15.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: AccelOpt: A Self-Improving LLM Agentic System for AI Accelerator Kernel Optimization
baseline_evidence: Claude Sonnet 4 (repeated sampling), kernels iniciais, beam search only (sem memória)
performance_evidence: +12pp throughput médio no Trainium 1 (49%→61%); +14pp no Trainium 2 (45%→59%) vs kernels iniciais; equivalente a Claude Sonnet 4
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Agent KB: Leveraging Cross-Domain Experience for Agentic Problem Solving
baseline_evidence: Baseline agent frameworks without AGENT KB, AGENT KB pass@1/pass@2/pass@3 variants, ablations without hybrid retrieval and feedback components, manually curated knowledge comparison.
performance_evidence: SWE-Agent GPT-4.1 improves 24.3% to 38.0% at 50 iterations (+13.7pp) and 27.0% to 42.3% at 100 iterations (+15.3pp). OpenHands Claude-3.7 improves 30.0% to 51.0% at 50 iterations (+21.0pp) and 41.3% to 53.3% at 100 iterations (+12.0pp).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: AlphaOPT: Formulating Optimization Programs with Self-Improving LLM Experience Library
baseline_evidence: Standard prompting, OptiMus, ORThought, ORLM, LLMOPT, Reflexion, ExpeL, AlphaOPT ablations without refinement and without insight examples.
performance_evidence: AlphaOPT exceeds ExpeL on test micro/macro averages (80.1/76.4 vs 69.9/66.3) and out-of-distribution micro/macro averages (86.5/74.8 vs 74.3/64.1). Removing refinement reduces out-of-distribution macro from 74.8 to 70.5.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Autonomous Code Evolution Meets NP-Completeness
baseline_evidence: Human-designed SAT Competition 2024 solvers; SAT Competition 2025 winner and runner-up; AlphaEvolve comparison at capability level.
performance_evidence: On the SAT Competition 2025 comparison, the top evolved solvers solve 347, 345, and 344 instances, compared with 334 and 331 instances for the gold and silver 2025 winners; figures report lowest PAR-2 scores on 2025 and 2024 benchmarks.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence does not document a no-persistence or non-memory baseline suitable for the controlled-comparison denominator.

title: CodeDistiller: Automatically Generating Code Libraries for Scientific Coding Agents
baseline_evidence: Different CodeDistiller base models; downstream CodeScientist with generic materials-science examples versus CodeDistiller-generated examples.
performance_evidence: Downstream A/B preference proportions favor CodeDistiller over the generic-example baseline for accuracy (0.50 vs 0.26), completeness (0.46 vs 0.16), and soundness (0.50 vs 0.30), with ties making up the remainder.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Confucius Code Agent: Scalable Agent Scaffolding for Real-World Codebases
baseline_evidence: SWE-Agent, Live-SWE-Agent, OpenHands, Anthropic (proprietário), OpenAI (proprietário)
performance_evidence: +3.0pp vs OpenAI (59.0% vs 56.0%) com GPT-5.2; +6.9pp vs Live-SWE-Agent (52.7% vs 45.8%) com Claude 4.5 Sonnet; +1.8pp vs OpenHands (74.6% vs 72.8%) no SWE-Bench-Verified
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
baseline_evidence: DGM without self-improving agents, DGM without open-ended exploration, checked open-source SWE-bench SoTA, Aider on Polyglot
performance_evidence: DGM outperforms variants without self-improvement and without open-ended exploration; best discovered SWE-bench agent is comparable to checked open-source human-designed SoTA, while Polyglot improves 14.2% to 30.7% on the full benchmark.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: ECO: An LLM-Driven Efficient Code Optimizer for Warehouse Scale Computers
baseline_evidence: Human baseline for microbenchmark edits; historical production changes; retrieval baselines including bag-of-words, deep text embeddings, and deep code embeddings; production before/after profiler measurements.
performance_evidence: Production deployment reports over 2M normalized cores saved, more than 500k normalized cores saved per quarter, and successful edits across multiple anti-pattern categories; ECO scales human optimization effort beyond manual search.
manual_rationale: PDF/text review found ECO comparisons against human edits, retrieval methods, unoptimized programs, and production before/after measures, but no clean no-persistence or non-memory coding-agent comparator.

title: EvoLattice: Persistent Internal-Population Evolution through Multi-Alternative Quality-Diversity Graph Representations for LLM-Guided Program Discovery
baseline_evidence: Spectral Energy, Covariance Trace, Spectral + Covariance fixed proxy, ZeroLM, LPZero, FunSearch-style proxy generation, AlphaEvolve-style diff evolution, ShinkaEvolve-like parent sampling.
performance_evidence: EvoLattice exceeds AlphaEvolve-style diff evolution (0.11 ±0.06), ShinkaEvolve-like parent sampling (0.12 ±0.05), FunSearch-style proxy generation (0.09 ±0.07), and LPZero (0.10 ±0.05).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Evolving Triple Knowledge-Augmented LLMs for Code Translation in Repository Context
baseline_evidence: Basic RAG adapted from prior work, prompt engineering baseline, C2Rust rule-based baseline, ablations over knowledge types and retrieval strategy.
performance_evidence: For Claude-3-5-Sonnet, K3 Trans improves over prompt engineering from 57.9% to 75.7% Compilation@1 and from 49.1% to 67.7% Pass@1; the paper reports relative improvements up to 135.9% on Pass@1 and 32.8% on CodeBLEU among studied LLMs.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: EXPEREPAIR: Dual-Memory Enhanced LLM-based Repository-Level Program Repair
baseline_evidence: SWE-Agent, Moatless Tools, Agentless-1.5, AutoCodeRover-v2, OpenHands, PatchPilot, DARS, and ablations without experience module, demonstrations, or insights.
performance_evidence: ExpeRepair surpasses PatchPilot 45.3% and DARS 47.0% on SWE-bench Lite while costing far less than DARS ($2.07 vs $12.24). Removing the experience module drops resolved rate from 47.7% to 41.3%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: From Knowledge to Noise: CTIM-Rover and the Pitfalls of Episodic Memory in Software Engineering Agents
baseline_evidence: AutoCodeRover (sem CTIM), configurações ablativas (CTIM only, General CTIM only, Repo-level CTIM only, Exemplar only)
performance_evidence: -2pp vs baseline (40% vs 42%); CTIM only -11pp (31% vs 42%)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Git Context Controller: Manage the Context of LLM-based Agents like Git
baseline_evidence: ReAct Agent, SWE Agent, Summary Agent, Folding Agent (controlados); 26 sistemas existentes em SWE-Benchlite (CodeStory Aide, MarsCode, Honeycomb, MentatBot, etc.)
performance_evidence: +6.2pp Pass@1 vs Folding Agent (melhor baseline) com Claude 4 Sonnet em SWE-Bench Verified (0.802 vs 0.740); +1.9pp BrowseComp-Plus com GPT-5 (0.834 vs 0.815)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Huxley-Gödel Machine: Human-Level Coding Agent Development by an Approximation of the Optimal Self-Improving Machine
baseline_evidence: SICA, DGM, initial ancestor, SWE-agent + GPT-5-mini, SWE-agent leaderboard baseline, HGM transfer variants.
performance_evidence: HGM requires 517 CPU-hours versus DGM 1231 on SWE-Verified-60, and 347 versus DGM 2385 on Polyglot; on SWE-Lite standard, the HGM best-belief agent reaches 49.0% with GPT-5-mini and 57.0% with GPT-5 transfer.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Improving Code Localization with Repository Memory
baseline_evidence: CodeRankEmbed, Agentless, LocAgent (sem memória)
performance_evidence: +4.9% Acc@5 absoluto vs LocAgent em SWE-bench-verified; +3.1% em SWE-bench-live; resolve rate +3.4% em end-to-end (40.4% vs 37.0%)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: KernelEvolve: Scaling Agentic Kernel Coding for Heterogeneous AI Accelerators at Meta
baseline_evidence: PyTorch baselines, expert manual implementations, existing kernel-generation prototypes such as KernelBench/KernelLLM/CWM-related systems.
performance_evidence: Reports 1.2x to 17x speedups over PyTorch baselines across production use cases and competitive performance with expert manual implementations.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence does not document a no-persistence or non-memory baseline suitable for the controlled-comparison denominator.

title: Lingxi: Repository-Level Issue Resolution Framework Enhanced by Procedural Knowledge Guided Scaling
baseline_evidence: mini-SWE-agent, OpenHands, SWE-agent, SWE-search, Augment Agent prior, raw issue-patch memory and no-knowledge ablations
performance_evidence: With Claude 4 Sonnet, Lingxi reports 74.60% versus OpenHands and Augment Agent prior at 70.40%, SWE-search at 70.08%, SWE-agent at 66.60%, and mini-SWE-agent at 64.93%. With Kimi-K2, Lingxi reports 70.33% versus OpenHands 65.40% and mini-SWE-agent 43.80%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Multi-Agent Code-Orchestrated Generation for Reliable Infrastructure-as-Code
baseline_evidence: Few-shot, chain-of-thought, multi-turn, RAG, and MACOG ablations without specialized agents/components.
performance_evidence: GPT-5 MACOG reaches 74.02 IaC-Eval, BLEU 11.86, CodeBERTScore 80.54, and LLM-judge 94.10. Ablations reduce IaC-Eval, including -Engineer at 64.89, -Security Prover at 61.45, -DevOps at 56.93, and -Memory Curator at 72.17.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: ReasoningBank: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
baseline_evidence: No Memory, Synapse, AWM and success-only/failure-trajectory ablations
performance_evidence: On SWE-bench Verified, Gemini-2.5-flash ReasoningBank reports 38.8% resolve versus 34.2% No Memory and 35.4% Synapse; Gemini-2.5-pro reports 57.4% versus 54.0% No Memory and 53.4% Synapse. Failure-trajectory ablations report larger gains than success-only memory variants.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: RedCodeAgent: Automatic Red-teaming Agent against Diverse Code Agents
baseline_evidence: No Jailbreak, GCG, AmpleGCG, Advprompter, AutoDAN, retry variants, and multiple target code agents such as OCI, RA, Cursor, Codeium, and MetaGPT
performance_evidence: Compared with no-jailbreak and jailbreak baselines, RedCodeAgent reports higher ASR across RedCode-Exec, RedCode-Gen, and RMCbench. In selected subtasks it reaches 70.00% ASR/20.00% RR for deleting sensitive files and 93.33% ASR/6.67% RR for adding risky aliases.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence does not document a no-persistence or non-memory baseline suitable for the controlled-comparison denominator.

title: Reflection-Driven Control for Trustworthy Code Agents
baseline_evidence: Base models without Reflex versus Base+Reflex across gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-4o, qwen3-coder-plus, and gemini-2.5-pro
performance_evidence: Base+Reflex improves security rate by +2.9 for gpt-3.5-turbo, +9.3 for gpt-4o, +11.2 for qwen3-coder-plus, and +9.1 for gemini-2.5-pro; pass rate is mostly preserved but decreases for qwen3-coder-plus (86.7 to 80.1).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Smarter Together: Creating Agentic Communities of Practice through Shared Experiential Learning
baseline_evidence: NO-SPARK (LLMs sem memória externa), DS-1000 Human Reference solutions (4.28/5 média)
performance_evidence: +0.66 code quality Qwen3-Coder (4.23→4.89); +0.41 Haiku 4.5 (4.50→4.91); +0.05 GPT-5-Codex (4.78→4.83); todos WITH-SPARK excedem qualidade humana (4.28)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: SWE-Exp: Experience-Driven Software Issue Resolution
baseline_evidence: Agentless, SWE-Agent, SWE-Search, OpenHands, EvoCoder, SAGE
performance_evidence: +2.2pp vs SWE-Search com Claude 4 Sonnet (73.0% vs 70.8%); +6.6pp vs SWE-Search com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 35.4%); +3.2pp vs SWE-Agent com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.8%); +4.2pp vs EvoCoder com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.0%)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: TAI3: Testing Agent Integrity in Interpreting User Intent
baseline_evidence: SelfRef baseline and ablations/generalization settings with different testing and target models
performance_evidence: Table 1 reports broad EESR gains over SelfRef, including Finance GPT-4o-mini VALID 41.5 to 61.0 (+19.5), Healthcare GPT-4o-mini INVALID 44.7 to 57.4 (+12.7), and Office Llama-3.1-8B UNDERSPEC 65.7 to 82.0 (+16.3).
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: TALM: Dynamic Tree-Structured Multi-Agent Framework with Long-Term Memory for Scalable Code Generation
baseline_evidence: Direct Prompting, Chain-of-Thought (CoT), Self-Planning Prompting, Reflexion, MapCoder
performance_evidence: +3.17pp BigCodeBench vs Direct (53.27% vs 50.10% GPT-4o); +5.00pp ClassEval class vs Direct (38.00% vs 33.00%); +2.00pp ClassEval class vs MapCoder (38.00% vs 36.00%); ~50% menos tokens que MapCoder
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: TOM-SWE: User Mental Modeling For Software Engineering Agents
baseline_evidence: CodeAct (vanilla sem memória), RAGCodeAct (single-agent com RAG de sessões anteriores)
performance_evidence: +11.5pp Ambiguous SWE-bench (63.4% vs 51.9%) com Claude Sonnet 4; +41.6pp Stateful SWE (59.7% vs 18.1%); satisfaction 3.62 vs 2.57 (+41%); RAGCodeAct inferior ao ToMCodeAct em todos modelos
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Toward Generation of Test Cases from Task Descriptions via History-aware Planning
baseline_evidence: WebGUM, RCI, Synapse, Li et al. staged LLM baseline with GPT-4o, and ablations without experience, without sequence-action memory, and without iterative planning
performance_evidence: On challenging MiniWoB++ tasks, HxAgent averages 82% Exact-Match versus 10% for Li et al. (GPT-4o). On real-world applications it averages 87% Exact-Match versus 28% and 93% Prefix-Match versus 34%. Removing experience drops Exact-Match from 97% to 88%; removing both SAM and experience drops it to 47%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Xolver: Multi-Agent Reasoning with Holistic Experience Learning Just Like an Olympiad Team
baseline_evidence: LongCoT (backbone direto), Self-Reflexion, OctoTools, Search-o1, CheatSheet, CodeSim; modelos proprietários (o1, o3, o4-mini-high, Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash); open-weight (DeepSeek-R1, Qwen3-235B); especializados (rStar-Math, AlphaOne, OpenMathReason, OpenCodeReason)
performance_evidence: +18.6pp AIME'24 avg vs o3-mini-medium LongCoT (93.8% vs 75.8%); +13.6pp AIME'25 (89.4% vs 75.8%); +21.0pp LiveCodeBench (87.3% vs 66.3%); supera o4-mini-high em AIME'24 (93.8% vs 93.4%) e LiveCodeBench (87.3% vs 69.5%)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: AdaExplore: Failure-Driven Adaptation and Diversity-Preserving Search for Efficient Kernel Generation
baseline_evidence: Single-pass GPT-5-mini/GPT-5/Claude-4.6-Opus, AutoTriton, DR. Kernel, parallel sampling, iterative refinement, OpenEvolve, variants with cross-task skill memory, and AdaExplore ablations
performance_evidence: At 50 steps on Level-2, AdaExplore reaches 2.65x speedup versus IR with skill memory at 2.59x and PS with skill memory at 2.12x. Removing cross-task skill memory drops speedup from 2.65x to 2.32x and Fast@1.2 from 71% to 56%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: AgentDevel: Reframing Self-Evolving LLM Agents as Release Engineering
baseline_evidence: Initial base agent, SWE-agent, GPT-4o scaffolded baseline, CER_hybrid, DFS, and ablations without flip gate, without executable diagnosis, and with non-blind critic
performance_evidence: On SWE-bench Lite, AgentDevel reaches 22.0% versus base 11.0% and reported SWE-agent 18.0%. On WebArena ablation, the full pipeline keeps P2F regression rate at 3.1% with zero bad releases; removing the flip gate raises P2F to 14.8% and creates four bad releases.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space
baseline_evidence: AIDE benchmark reference; contextual comparisons to H2O AutoML, AutoGPT, and human with ChatGPT; Initial de novo proposals versus parent-conditioned operators
performance_evidence: Parent-conditioned operators win 48.7% of parent-child tournaments overall, while Initial proposals win only 12.2%. Continue wins 148/286 tournaments, Merge 68/104, EDA 267/593, Ablation 34/78, and Initial 9/74.
manual_rationale: PDF/text review found a direct initial/de novo proposal comparator against parent-conditioned operators using inherited archives.

title: APEX-EM: Non-Parametric Online Learning for Autonomous Agents via Structured Procedural-Episodic Experience Replay
baseline_evidence: No Memory, Pass@10, Reflexion, RAG, Self-RAG, Mem0, MemP, MemRL, semantic-only/entity-graph/full-memory ablations, Opus-judge ablation
performance_evidence: BigCodeBench improvement is +29.4pp over no memory and exceeds MemRL's reported +11.0pp gain. KGQA full-memory plus Opus judge reaches 89.6% versus 41.3% no memory. HLE entity graph retrieval improves 25.2% to 48.0%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: BEAM: Bi-level Memory-adaptive Algorithmic Evolution for LLM-Powered Heuristic Design
baseline_evidence: EoH, ReEvo, MCTS-AHD, AlphaEvolve, LLaMEA, LLaMEA-HPO, KaMIS, RLSA, ARW, HGS, Split and local search, EACO-EDM, BE without Adaptive Memory, one-shot and MCTS education baselines
performance_evidence: In CVRP, BEAM with Split and local search reports gaps of 0.09%, 0.38%, and 0.86% for CVRP-100/200/500 versus EoH gaps of 0.22%, 0.57%, and 1.09%. In BBOB, BEAM average gap is 0.007 versus ReEvo 0.957, EoH 6.032, and LLaMEA-HPO 0.386.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Beyond Crash-to-Patch: Patch Evolution for Linux Kernel Repair
baseline_evidence: Unguided baseline coding agent, memory-guided agent, base advisors without fine-tuning, fine-tuned PatchAdvisor variants
performance_evidence: Baseline repair succeeds on 1/6 bugs with CodeBERTScore 0.42 and 83.3% compile success. Memory-guided repair succeeds on 4/6 with CodeBERTScore 0.74. Fine-tuned Gemma-3-12B PatchAdvisor succeeds on 5/6 with average rank 2.7.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Co-RedTeam: Orchestrated Security Discovery and Exploitation with LLM Agents
baseline_evidence: Vanilla prompting, OpenHands, C-Agent, VulTrail, RepoAudit, component ablations without critique, validation, vulnerability documents, code browser, memory, or execution
performance_evidence: For Gemini-3-pro, OpenHands reaches 45.2%/45.0%/5.0%/20.2% across CyBench, BountyBench exploit/detect, and CyberGym; Co-RedTeam reaches 63.7%/65.0%/20.0%/37.3%. Removing memory drops Gemini-2.5-pro CyBench from 59.1% to 50.0%, BountyBench exploit from 60.0% to 40.0%, and CyberGym from 31.5% to 22.6%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Codebase-Memory: Tree-Sitter-Based Knowledge Graphs for LLM Code Exploration via MCP
baseline_evidence: File-exploration agent using conventional file reading and grep; comparison to embedding/RAG, RepoMap, and graph-plus-LLM retrieval approaches
performance_evidence: MCP Agent reaches 90% of Explorer quality while using 10 times fewer tokens and 2.1 times fewer tool calls; it matches or exceeds explorer behavior on graph-native hub/caller queries for 19 of 31 languages.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: CoEvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
baseline_evidence: No-skill baseline, Anthropic Skill-Creator, single-session skill generation, SkillsBench self-generated skills, CoT-guided self-generation, human-curated skills, ablations without surrogate verifier or without evolution
performance_evidence: CoEvoSkills improves +40.5pp over no-skill and +17.6pp over human-curated skills. Skill-Creator reaches 34.1%, single-session generation 32.4%, SkillsBench self-generated 32.0%, and CoT-guided self-generation 30.7%. Removing the surrogate verifier drops pass rate from 71.1% to 41.1%; removing evolution drops to 48.6%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
baseline_evidence: Outcome-based RL baseline without experience, Static Online Experience, Static Online Experience evaluated without experience, Experience Only, Complementary RL with/without experience at evaluation
performance_evidence: Multi-task table reports baseline average 0.75, Complementary RL evaluated with experience 0.82, and Complementary RL without experience 0.78. Static Online Experience with experience averages 0.59 and without experience 0.54, showing that static experience alone is insufficient.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Controlled Self-Evolution for Algorithmic Code Optimization
baseline_evidence: Direct generation, Self-Reflection, SE-Agent, AlphaEvolve, ablations without diversified planning, genetic evolution, or hierarchical memory
performance_evidence: With GPT-5, CSE improves average MI to 67.47% versus Direct 59.13%, Self-Reflection 60.24%, SE-Agent 64.41%, and AlphaEvolve 63.04%. Ablation on GPT-5 shows full CSE MI 68.10% versus 63.08% without memory, 63.82% without planning, and 64.72% without evolution.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
baseline_evidence: OpenEvolve, ShinkaEvolve, EvoX, single-agent CORAL, independent best-of-four single-agent runs, no-knowledge ablation
performance_evidence: CORAL improvement rates are reported as 3-10 times higher than fixed evolutionary-search baselines and typically converge within 5-20 evaluations versus 60-100 for baselines. Knowledge ablation worsens Kernel Engineering from 1350 to 1601 cycles, Polyominoes from 80.2 to 77.3, and Txn Scheduling from 4566 to 4444.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: DARWIN: Dynamic Agentically Rewriting Self-Improving Network
baseline_evidence: Baseline nanoGPT configuration and ablation without the memory file system
performance_evidence: The paper reports a 2.07 percent reduction in perplexity and a 1.26 percent reduction in MFU; removing persistent memory produced about 3 percent worse performance, but the authors caution that the results are not statistically meaningful.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Decocted Experience Improves Test-Time Inference in LLM Agents
baseline_evidence: Vanilla agent without context, raw experience retrieval, distilled lesson retrieval, full memory, k-means consolidated memory, flat top-k lesson retrieval, hierarchical concept tree
performance_evidence: On WebShop and SWE, lesson-based context consistently outperforms raw trajectories and vanilla context-free agents. Concept-tree retrieval improves over top-k lesson retrieval on WebShop and shows a noticeable improvement on SWE, while excessive K/raw context can degrade performance.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: DockSmith: Scaling Reliable Coding Environments via an Agentic Docker Builder
baseline_evidence: DeepSeek-v3.1, DeepSeek-R1, GPT-OSS-20B, GPT-OSS-120B, Kimi-K2 variants, Qwen3-235B-A22B, Claude-Sonnet-4, GPT-5-Mini, Gemini-2.5-Flash, Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct, SWE-only and Docker-only training ablations, acceptance-shaping and curriculum ablations
performance_evidence: DockSmith improves +20.3 absolute F2P points over Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct. Error analysis reduces total errors from 3,757 to 2,161, critical errors by 40.2%, and average errors per trajectory from 11.81 to 6.67.
manual_rationale: PDF/text review found model, training, acceptance-shaping, and curriculum comparisons, but no clean no-memory or no-success-memory ablation for DockSmith's persistence mechanism.

title: DomAgent: Leveraging Knowledge Graphs and Case-Based Reasoning for Domain-Specific Code Generation
baseline_evidence: Vanilla Qwen2.5-7B, LLaMA3.1-8B, LLaMA3.3-70B, GPT-4o, KG-only, CBR-only, KG+CBR RAG pipeline, StarCoder, WizardCoder, CodeLLaMA-Python, WizardCoder-CL, Magicoder-CL, MagicoderS-CL, random case sampling
performance_evidence: On Truck CAN Signal, Qwen2.5-7B improves from 39.62% to 96.64% total pass@1, and GPT-4o with DomRetriever improves from 71.22% to 98.04%. On DS-1000, Qwen2.5-7B improves from 29.3% to 39.2% total pass@1; external GPT-4o improves from 51.0% to 58.6% with LLaMA3.1-8B DomRetriever.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: EET: Experience-Driven Early Termination for Cost-Efficient Software Engineering Agents
baseline_evidence: Agentes sem EET (Agentless, Mini-SWE-Agent, Trae Agent), Turn-control (Gao and Peng 2026), Naive-EET (RAG com trajetórias brutas)
performance_evidence: +2.7% resolve rate médio vs agentes sem EET; Agentless beneficia mais (+7.5% abs com GPT-5-mini)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: EffiSkill: Agent Skill Based Automated Code Efficiency Optimization
baseline_evidence: Instruction, RAG, CoT, SBLLM, FasterPy, EffiCoder, ablations without retrieval, with random skills, and without multi-plan composition
performance_evidence: With GPT-5-mini, EffiSkill reaches Python OPT@1/OPT@8 of 26.48/37.40 and C++ OPT@1/OPT@8 of 44.62/66.77. With Qwen3-Coder, it reaches Python 21.35/36.60 and C++ 34.19/56.50, outperforming Instruction, RAG, CoT, SBLLM, FasterPy, and EffiCoder where applicable.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: FailureMem: A Failure-Aware Multimodal Framework for Autonomous Software Repair
baseline_evidence: GUIRepair, Agentless Lite, SWE-agent, Zencoder, Computer-Use Agents, commercial systems, and FailureMem ablations
performance_evidence: FailureMem reaches 31.1%, 33.1%, 33.3%, 32.5%, and 33.8% resolved rates with GPT-4.1, GPT-5.1, GPT-5.2, Claude 4, and Claude 4.5 respectively, compared with GUIRepair at 28.8%, 29.4%, 29.8%, 28.6%, and 31.5%.
manual_rationale: PDF/text review found explicit memory-free/no-historical-memory FailureMem comparators and component ablations.

title: Group-Evolving Agents: Open-Ended Self-Improvement via Experience Sharing
baseline_evidence: DGM-like self-evolving baseline, Darwin Godel Machine lineage, OpenHands+GPT-5, Aider+GPT-5, human-designed framework comparisons
performance_evidence: GEA nearly matches OpenHands+GPT-5 on SWE-bench Verified (71.0% versus 71.8%) and exceeds Aider+GPT-5 on Polyglot (88.3% versus 52.0%). It fixes framework-level bugs in 1.4 iterations on average versus 5.0 for DGM trials.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence does not document a no-persistence or non-memory baseline suitable for the controlled-comparison denominator.

title: KAPSO: A Knowledge-grounded framework for Autonomous Program Synthesis and Optimization
baseline_evidence: R&D-Agent, AIRA-dojo, ML-Master, AIDE (MLE-Bench); ALE-Agent, ALE Sequential, ALE one-shot (ALE-Bench)
performance_evidence: +15.56pp medal rate vs R&D-Agent em MLE-Bench; +30.1 ELO vs ALE-Agent em ALE-Bench; vantagem concentrada em tarefas Hard (+17.78pp)
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: KernelBlaster: Continual Cross-Task CUDA Optimization via Memory-Augmented In-Context Reinforcement Learning
baseline_evidence: AI CUDA Engineer, IREE compiler, PyTorch (Eager + torch.compile), Kernelsseum (zero-shot), no_mem_agent (ablação)
performance_evidence: GeoMean 1.43× (L1), 2.50× (L2), 1.50× (L3) vs PyTorch; 1.67× vs no_mem_agent; supera AI CUDA Engineer em fast_p para speedups moderados a altos
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: KernelSkill: A Multi-Agent Framework for GPU Kernel Optimization
baseline_evidence: Kevin-32B, Astra, PRAGMA, CudaForge, QiMeng, STARK, Torch Eager, and KernelSkill ablations
performance_evidence: Compared with STARK, KernelSkill improves average speedup by 79.5% on Level 1, 4.8% on Level 2, and 21.5% on Level 3. It reaches these gains with 15 refinement rounds, while STARK reports 30 rounds.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Large Language Model-Powered Evolutionary Code Optimization on a Phylogenetic Tree
baseline_evidence: Baseline implementations and evolutionary search variants for the scientific-code optimization workloads
performance_evidence: The paper reports improvements in runtime performance, memory efficiency, and correctness over baseline and evolutionary methods, including reusable motifs such as kernel fusion, shared-memory tiling, and batching strategies.
manual_rationale: PDF/text review found baseline implementation and evolutionary-search comparisons, but no clean no-persistence or no-trajectory-memory comparator for PhyloEvolve.

title: LatentMem: Customizing Latent Memory for Multi-Agent Systems
baseline_evidence: No-memory, MetaGPT, Generative, Voyager, G-Memory, OAgent/OAgents, JoyAgent, ChatDev, MARTI fine-tuning baseline
performance_evidence: In Table 4 with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, LatentMem improves AutoGen average from 52.86 to 61.82, MacNet from 53.38 to 61.00, CAMEL from 53.37 to 60.04, and DyLAN from 52.80 to 60.06; KodCode gains range from +14.90 to +18.45 points.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Learning to Commit: Generating Organic Pull Requests via Online Repository Memory
baseline_evidence: Base agent without skill document under the same Claude Opus 4.6 tool-use capabilities
performance_evidence: Table 2 reports File IoU improvements in three of four settings, including 80% versus 61% in seq-all and 80% versus 68% in par-bycat. Table 3 reports skill-agent win rates of 54%/57% in par-bycat and 55%/58% in seq-all under Claude/Gemini judges.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: MEMCoder: Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory for Private-Library-Oriented Code Generation
baseline_evidence: Naive RAG, EpiGen, CAPIR, ReMem, DC-RS, MEMCoder ablations, and oracle static documentation analysis
performance_evidence: With Qwen2.5-Coder, Naive RAG plus MEMCoder reaches 52.54 Pass@1 on NdonnxEval and 33.74 on NumbaEval versus Naive RAG at 27.22 and 23.16. In continual-learning comparison, MEMCoder reaches 33.74 Pass@1 on NumbaEval versus ReMem 23.16 and DC-RS 17.33.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: MemCollab: Cross-Agent Memory Collaboration via Contrastive Trajectory Distillation
baseline_evidence: Vanilla agent, Buffer of Thoughts, Dynamic Cheatsheet, memory from same model, memory from another model, self-contrast memory
performance_evidence: For Qwen2.5-7B, MemCollab improves average accuracy from 57.1 to 71.6; MBPP improves from 47.9 to 57.6 and HumanEval from 42.7 to 74.4. For Qwen2.5-32B, average accuracy improves from 70.8 to 79.6.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: MemGovern: Enhancing Code Agents through Learning from Governed Human Experiences
baseline_evidence: SWE-Agent (sem memória), AutoCodeRover, CodeAct, SWESynInfer, mini-SWE-agent, RAG estático, Agentic RAG
performance_evidence: +3.2pp com Claude 4 Sonnet (69.8% vs 66.6%); +8.2pp com Qwen3-235B (55.4% vs 47.2%); +9.4pp com GPT-4o (32.6% vs 23.2%); média +4.65pp em 7 LLMs
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
baseline_evidence: Zero-shot coding agent, ReasoningBank, AgentKB, memory retrieval variants with LLM reranking and adaptive rewriting
performance_evidence: With GPT-5-mini, zero-shot average Pass@3 is 0.523 and MTL with Insight reaches 0.560. MTL outperforms ReasoningBank and AgentKB on the three-benchmark comparison with average 0.630 versus 0.601 and 0.613.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: ODEL: An Experience-Augmented Self-Evolving Framework for Efficient Python-to-C++ Code Translation
baseline_evidence: Qwen2.5-Coder single-agent, UniTrans Method, Multi-Agent no-experience, ODEL internal experience, Deepseek-Coder single-agent, Deepseek-Coder ODEL
performance_evidence: ODEL External Experience outperforms UniTrans at 72.16% Pass@1 and 76.64% Pass@10. It also improves over ODEL Internal Experience by 6.35 Pass@1 points and 4.32 Pass@10 points.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: OpenGame: Open Agentic Coding for Games
baseline_evidence: Direct LLMs, qwen-code, Cursor, Qwen-3.5-27B, GameCoder-27B, ablations without hook-driven implementation, three-layer reading, physics-first classification, evolved templates, and living debug protocol
performance_evidence: OpenGame with Claude Sonnet 4.6 outperforms Cursor with Claude Sonnet 4.6 by 5.6 Build Health, 5.8 Visual Usability, and 6.2 Intent Alignment points. GameCoder-27B reaches 63.9/57.0/54.1 and outperforms every direct LLM baseline on Build Health and Intent Alignment.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: OpenSage: Self-programming Agent Generation Engine
baseline_evidence: Anthropic Agent, OpenHands, Droid, Simple Codex, Claude Code, SWE-agent, Agentless, Mem0g, NoMem, OpenSage feature ablations
performance_evidence: On SWE-Bench Pro, SageAgent with Gemini 3 Flash reaches 59.0% versus SWE-agent 40.2% and Agentless 9.4%. In the memory ablation, OpenSage reaches 59.0 versus Mem0g 56.4 and NoMem 56.2.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Outcome-Conditioned Reasoning Distillation for Resolving Software Issues
baseline_evidence: Agentless, SWE-Agent, OpenHands, AutoCodeRover, MoatlessTools, MASAI, AutoCodeRover-v2, SWE-Search, O-CRD without Exemplar Guardian, O-CRD with MCTS-style forward search
performance_evidence: With GPT-4o, O-CRD improves over Agentless from 27.3% to 37.7% Pass@1 and exceeds SWE-Search by 6.7 percentage points. With GPT-5, it improves Agentless from 29.7% to 40.0%; with DeepSeek-V3, from 27.0% to 35.6%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Procedural Knowledge at Scale Improves Reasoning
baseline_evidence: No RAG, Trajectory RAG prefix, Trajectory RAG summary, Template RAG ReasonFlux, Template RAG Self-Discover, Document RAG CompactDS, Document RAG Google, retrieval-free Length Scaling
performance_evidence: On LiveCodeBench V1-4 with Qwen3-32B and m=30, Reasoning Memory reaches 0.471 versus Length Scaling 0.405 and No RAG 0.387 at m=8; on LiveCodeBench V5-6 it reaches 0.508 versus Length Scaling 0.476 and No RAG 0.465.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: ReCreate: Reasoning and Creating Domain Agents Driven by Experience
baseline_evidence: CoT, Step-Back Abstraction, Self-Refine, LIVE, OPRO, ExpeL, ADAS, AgentSquare, ReCreate without creation router, ReCreate without domain update
performance_evidence: On SWE subsets, ReCreate reaches 60.19 on Django and 63.64 on SymPy, exceeding the best baseline in each row. It also improves over ADAS and AgentSquare averages by more than seven points.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: REVERE: Reflective Evolving Research Engineer for Scientific Workflows
baseline_evidence: Minimal baseline prompts, Static SOTA prompts, GEPA, ACE, REVERE without cheatsheet, REVERE without auxiliary context, REVERE without reflection history, REVERE without Global Training Context
performance_evidence: REVERE improves SUPER Overall by +4.50 over Static SOTA, ResearchCodeBench Accuracy by +1.3, and ScienceAgentBench SuccessRate by +4.89; removing the full Global Training Context drops the representative SUPER Overall from 34.7 to 16.0.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: RuleFlow: Generating Reusable Program Optimizations with LLMs
baseline_evidence: DIAS, MODIN, DASK, KOALAS, single-agent RuleGen ablation
performance_evidence: RuleFlow reaches a maximum speedup of 4.3x over DIAS and 1914.89x over MODIN; compared with a single-agent RuleGen variant, the multi-agent RuleGen obtains 68% valid rules versus 18%.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Scaling Teams or Scaling Time? Memory Enabled Lifelong Learning in LLM Multi-Agent Systems
baseline_evidence: No memory, MARBLE memory-enabled MultiAgentBench baseline, A-Mem dynamic memory framework, LLMA-Mem topology variants, LLMA-Mem consolidation-interval variants
performance_evidence: In the coding topology ablation with Qwen3-32B-Instruct, local memory obtains TS 49.68, CS 46.69, and AAS 49.43 versus shared 46.79/45.96/48.62 and hybrid 46.52/40.32/46.92.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: SEVerA: Verified Self-Evolving Agents
baseline_evidence: LLM baselines, constrained decoding methods, Agent-C, and SEVerA ablations over verification/learning components
performance_evidence: SEVerA exceeds the best constrained-decoding method on GSM-Symbolic by 21.3 percentage points and reports 52.6% on tau2-bench airline versus 47.3% for Agent-C with Claude Sonnet 4.5.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Software Self-Extension with SelfEvolve: an Agentic Architecture for Runtime Code Generation
baseline_evidence: AgentCoder, AutoGen, MetaGPT, and SelfEvolve without TDD
performance_evidence: SelfEvolve reaches 92.7% Pass@1 versus AutoGen at 30.9%, MetaGPT at 27.3%, and AgentCoder at 0.0%. Removing TDD reduces performance to 72.7% and raises average iterations from 2.2 to 4.7.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence reports quantitative comparison against external or generic systems without the reviewed persistence architecture; count separately from strict ablations if needed.

title: Structurally Aligned Subtask-Level Memory for Software Engineering Agents
baseline_evidence: Vanilla Agent (Mini SWE Agent sem memória), Instance-level Memory (reprodução de ReasoningBank)
performance_evidence: +6.8pp Gemini 2.5 Pro (60.3% vs 53.5%); +5.6pp Gemini 2.5 Flash (41.9% vs 36.3%); +3.9pp Claude 3.7 Sonnet (56.1% vs 52.2%); +2.3pp Claude 4.0 Sonnet (65.8% vs 63.5%); média +4.7pp
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: SuperLocalMemory V3.3: The Living Brain -- Biologically-Inspired Forgetting, Cognitive Quantization, and Multi-Channel Retrieval for Zero-LLM Agent Memory Systems
baseline_evidence: Cosine retrieval baseline, SuperLocalMemory V3.2 baseline, LoCoMo baselines and Mode C variants
performance_evidence: The paper reports +23.8 percentage points on multi-hop, +15.3 on temporal, and +12.7 on adversarial questions versus the V3.3 baseline, while V3.3 trails V3.2 overall LoCoMo Mode A by 4.4 percentage points due to fusion complexity.

title: TextBFGS: A Case-Based Reasoning Approach to Code Optimization via Error-Operator Retrieval
baseline_evidence: TextGrad, TextGrad-Momentum, TextBFGS without case base, and TextBFGS-REMO with problem-based retrieval
performance_evidence: On HumanEval-Hard, TextBFGS reaches 97.78/93.33 versus 91.11/82.22 for TextBFGS without case base and 95.56/91.11 for REMO. Cross-domain TextBFGS maintains 74.36 MBPP Plus Pass using a HumanEval case base versus 58.12 for REMO.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Toward Autonomous Long-Horizon Engineering for ML Research
baseline_evidence: BasicAgent, IterAgent, AiScientist without File-as-Bus, and MLE-Bench Lite leaderboard/baseline systems
performance_evidence: On PaperBench, AiScientist improves over IterAgent by 9.92 points with Gemini and 11.36 points with GLM-5. Removing File-as-Bus drops PaperBench by 6.41 points and MLE-Bench Lite any-medal rate by 31.82 points.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
baseline_evidence: OpenHands CodeActAgent (gpt-4o native), +Global guardrail only, +GBT-Basic, +GBT-SE (ablation ladder progressiva)
performance_evidence: +39.0pp SWE-bench (73.6% vs 34.6%); +47.2pp WebArena (66.9% vs 19.7%); +28.5pp GPQA (87.3% vs 58.7%); violations 2.8%→0.2% SWE-bench; unsafe success 1.2%→0.0%

title: When the Specification Emerges: Benchmarking Faithfulness Loss in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
baseline_evidence: Emergent specification runs, single-shot specification control, and ProjectGuard mitigation on Claude Code/Codex
performance_evidence: Single-shot outperforms emergent specification on 16 of 20 papers for Claude Code and 14 of 20 for Codex. ProjectGuard improves Claude IF50 on 15 of 20 papers; Codex semantic gains are small but DIR improves on 15 of 19 analyzed cases.
manual_rationale: Extracted evidence names a no-memory/no-persistence comparator or a memory-component ablation.

title: Your Code Agent Can Grow Alongside You with Structured Memory
baseline_evidence: OpenHands (Claude Opus 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.5, GPT-5.2, Gemini 3 Pro), TRAE, DaubanCode, Live-SWE-agent, EPAM AI/Run, Atlassian Rover, ACoder, Warp
performance_evidence: +9.4pp vs sem memória com DeepSeek-V3.2 (77.8% vs 68.4%); +3.4pp vs OpenHands+GPT-5.2 (78.8% vs ~74.4%); top-6 no leaderboard SWE-bench Verified

title: Self-Adapting Agents for Automating Research Coding Workflows
baseline_evidence: Baseline agent, Static SOTA, GEPA-style adaptation, SARE without ground truth, SARE with ground truth, and SARE ablations without cheatsheet, auxiliary context, reflection, or Global Training Context
performance_evidence: SARE without ground truth improves the SUPER overall score to 38.8 versus 29.9 for the baseline, while SARE with ground truth reaches 52.8. Ablating the whole Global Training Context drops SUPER overall from 34.7 to 16.0 in the reported ablation table.
